sections.paragraph:
κόπρον, ου, τό (s. two prec. entries and next entry; Galen XII 290 K.—For the LXX ἡ κόπρος [s. the foll. entry] is surely correct wherever the gender can be established. But there is also the acc. κόπρον which, without the article, may be fem. or neut. [Is 30:22; 36:12, which latter form has τήν w. it as a v.l.]; TestBenj 8:3; ApcMos 6; and likew. the gen. κόπρου [4 Km 6:25; Ezk 4:12; Jos., Ant. 9, 62; PGM 7, 485]) Hs 9, 10, 3 if the restoration τὰ κό[πρα] in FXFunk2 1901 is correct, and perh. κόπρον Lk 13:8 v.l. But the latter form more likely belongs under the next entry.

prec. prec. = preceding

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

foll. foll. = followed, following

acc. acc. = accusative

fem. fem. = feminine

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcMos ApcMos = Apocalypse of Moses—List 2

likew. likew. = likewise

gen. gen. = genitive

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Hs Hs = Similitudes

perh. perh. = perhaps

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 559
